claim: Gemini 3 leads five of ten independent benchmarks and improves on humanity's last exam by 10 percentage points, largest gap between Gemini 2.5 and 3.0 ever seen
actionable: Update benchmark tracking; Gemini 3 is the new state-of-the-art, especially for reasoning and multimodal tasks

claim: Scaling laws are alive; continuing to improve pre-training and post-training yields drastic jumps in capability, contradicting narratives of scaling plateau
actionable: Treat scaling laws as still valid; expect continued capability improvements from parameter scaling and training refinements

claim: Gemini 3 is extremely token-efficient; intelligence per token is increasing rapidly, critical for autonomous agent duration (more work per token spent)
actionable: Evaluate models on intelligence-per-token, not just raw capability; token efficiency determines agent runtime and cost

claim: Gemini 3 dominates two of three coding benchmarks and excels in agentic coding scenarios, signaling stronger agent orchestration and tool use
actionable: For agentic coding, prioritize Gemini 3; it is optimized for multi-step tool-calling workflows

claim: Google's stack ownership (custom silicon, training infrastructure, distribution via Android) gives them competitive advantage; they own entire AI stack
actionable: Recognize Google's structural advantage; competitors must build partnerships to compete on infrastructure, not just models

claim: Gemini 3 improves dramatically on ARC v2 (reasoning) but does not proportionally improve on ARC v1, suggesting models still fail on easy tasks despite excelling on hard ones
actionable: Use diverse benchmark sets; large improvements on hard benchmarks may mask persistent failures on simple generalization tasks
